input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 targeting Android + Windows.

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK (latest with MAUI support)

```powershell
# Download and run the official installer
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --list-sdks
```

## 2. Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# Discover available workloads dynamically
dotnet workload search maui

# Install the MAUI workload (includes Android, Windows targets)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target dependency)

Windows targeting requires the Windows App SDK and WinUI tooling, which come with VS or the Build Tools:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# During install or after, add required workload components:
# Use the VS Installer CLI to modify:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --quiet --wait
```

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android build dependency)

```powershell
# MAUI for Android needs JDK 17
winget search Microsoft.OpenJDK

# Install JDK 17
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17

# Verify
java -version

# Set JAVA_HOME if not auto-set
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "JAVA_HOME",
  "C:\Program Files\Microsoft\jdk-17*" -replace '\*',
    (Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files\Microsoft" -Filter "jdk-17*" |
     Select-Object -First 1).Name,
  "User"
)
```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Accept Licenses

```powershell
# The MAUI workload installs Android SDK pieces under:
$androidSdkPath = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# If not present, find where dotnet provisioned it:
dotnet workload list  # shows android-related packs

# Dynamically discover installed Android components:
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install specific platform targets (discover latest dynamically):
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools"

# Set ANDROID_HOME
[System.Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable(
  "ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdkPath, "User"
)
```

## 6. Create & Build a Test Project

```powershell
# Discover available MAUI templates
dotnet new list maui

# Create a new project
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows (WinUI)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
```

## 7. Run on Each Target

```powershell
# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Discover available Android emulators
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -list-avds

# If none exist, create one:
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" `
  create avd -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Launch emulator
Start-Process "$env:ANDROID_HOME\emulator\emulator.exe" -ArgumentList "-avd Pixel_API_35"

# Deploy to emulator
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Validate the Full Toolchain

```powershell
# All-in-one diagnostic check
dotnet workload list
dotnet --info
java -version
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed
```

---

**Key notes:**
- Replace `net9.0-*` with your actual SDK version (`dotnet --version` to check). .NET 10 may be current by now — adjust accordingly.
- The `dotnet workload install maui` step pulls most Android SDK dependencies automatically.
- Windows targeting requires the **Windows App SDK**, which comes with the VS managed desktop workload.
- If you skip Visual Studio entirely, you'll need to install the [Windows App SDK](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/windows/apps/windows-app-sdk/) runtime separately.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They want PowerShell commands and dynamic version discovery. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
